summary:
Phase 5.A.2 (sandbox-verifiable renderer + input + ui implementation) complete. The simulation transitions from diagnostic-only to interactively playable: src/render/canvas.ts (226 lines, drawScene tile renderer reading sim state read-only with imageSmoothingEnabled=false nearest-neighbor blits and integer-only pixel math); src/input/keyboard.ts (171 lines, startKeyboard + keyEventToAction + DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS mapping arrow keys / WASD + Q/E/Z/C diagonals + Space.wait + Period.descend to the Phase 1 Action schema as configuration not schema); src/ui/hud.ts (87 lines, renderHud HP/floor/outcome/fingerprint widget reading RunState read-only); src/main.ts split into renderDiagnostic (preserves the entire Phase 1.A → 4.A.2 diagnostic surface verbatim, wrapped in
so existing e2e selectors resolve without expansion clicks) + startGame (atlas load → keyboard wiring → tick → render loop, with #game-error visible UI surface for atlas-loader refusals); five new window flags __GAME_READY__/__GAME_STATE_HASH__/__GAME_FLOOR__/__GAME_HP__/__GAME_OUTCOME__ for cross-runtime Playwright assertions, plus __GAME_ERROR__ for the failure-paths surface; eslint.config.js extended with src/render/**, src/input/**, src/ui/** scopes banning sim write paths (core/streams.ts, sim/combat.ts, sim/turn.ts, sim/run.ts, mapgen/generate.ts) plus cross-imports between the three layers and main.ts. Atlas-loader integration: src/main.ts:startGame() calls await loadAtlas(import.meta.env.BASE_URL + 'assets') before any sim tick, with all four addendum-N7 refusal paths active (PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION, ATLAS_MISSING, hash mismatch with em-dash U+2014, decode failure) surfaced via #game-error + window.__GAME_READY__ = 'error' + window.__GAME_ERROR__. Read-only contract enforced both at runtime (tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts deeply-freezes RunState + LoadedAtlas via recursive Object.freeze and asserts drawScene + renderHud do not throw) and at edit time (no-restricted-imports lint scope). 41 net-new unit tests (9 canvas + 2 readonly + 24 keyboard + 6 hud) bringing the total to 865 across 58 test files; all coverage thresholds met (src/render 96.61%/93.33% lines/branches with the 4 uncovered lines being defensive paths documented inline in vitest.config.ts; src/input 100%; src/ui 100%). 4 net-new e2e tests (playable-game section visible + __GAME_READY__ ready; HUD reflects initial RunState; five movement keys advance state hash; diagnostic surface preserved in
) bringing the total to 18 unique × 3 browsers = 54 cross-runtime assertions for Phase 5.B's CI Playwright run. Bundle 69.17 KB raw / 26.04 KB gzipped (was 24.03 KB; +2.01 KB for the renderer + input + HUD + atlas-image-decode; ~67% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget). Diagnostic surface preserved verbatim — all 16 existing DOM ids and all 12 existing window.__*__ flags emit unchanged, confirmed by direct grep of the live preview bundle. Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-2.md); 3 cosmetic suggestions (canvas.ts:150 fillStyle hidden constant, tests/ui/fake-dom.ts may eventually push toward happy-dom in Phase 6+, Direction cast in DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS could be a tiny helper) carried forward as non-blocking. qa-playwright equivalent: cannot run Playwright in-sandbox (no browser binaries, same constraint as Phase 3.A.2 / 4.A.1 / 5.A.1); verified via local vite preview (HTTP 200 + bundle structure probe) that all 5 new __GAME_* markers + all preserved diagnostic markers are present in the built bundle and that assets/atlas.png is served correctly with Content-Length 696 + sha256 matching ATLAS_DIGEST. Full cross-runtime verification on chromium/firefox/webkit deferred to Phase 5.B per the established X.B precedent.
suggested_commit_message: phase-5.A.2: renderer + input + HUD (atlas-loader integration + diagnostic→playable transition + 5 game window flags + 4 e2e tests)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 5.A.1 foundation (HEAD of phase-5.A.1 commit 71b596e) had not drifted before Phase 5.A.2 implementation began, and confirm the 5.A.2 implementation maintains the foundation's invariants. Phase 4 + Phase 5.A.1 were end-to-end verified previously.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Pre-phase: 824 passed across 54 test files at HEAD of phase-5.A.1. Post-phase: 865 passed across 58 test files. Net-new: +41 tests across 4 new test files (tests/render/canvas.test.ts +9, tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts +2, tests/input/keyboard.test.ts +24, tests/ui/hud.test.ts +6). Coverage thresholds met for the new src/render/** (95/85/100/95 with 4 uncovered defensive-path lines documented inline in vitest.config.ts:93-98), src/input/** (100/100/100/100), src/ui/** (100/100/100/100). Phase 1/2/3/4 coverage unchanged.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. New src/render/**, src/input/**, src/ui/** scope additions (no-restricted-imports patterns banning sim write paths + cross-layer imports + src/main; no-float-arithmetic plugin scope extended) verified empirically. The single-orchestrator rule (only src/main.ts wires the four layers together) holds.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors (TS strict + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noUncheckedIndexedAccess). All sim-state reads in the new layers use the existing Phase 3 `Readonly<...>` types from src/sim/types.ts; no value imports from sim write paths.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-*.js = 69.17 KB raw / 26.04 KB gzipped. The bundle delta from phase-5.A.1's 24.03 KB is +2.01 KB (renderer + input + HUD + atlas-image-decode pipeline added). 67% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget. dist/assets/atlas.png + dist/assets/atlas.json continue to be copied via the vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash plugin's closeBundle hook (Phase 4.A.2 carry).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Local vite preview (http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/) verified: GET /icefall/ returns HTTP 200 with the expected bundle script tag (index-B1JXfoiZ.js); GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 696 and sha256 d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a (matches ATLAS_DIGEST byte-for-byte and the in-repo assets/atlas.png). Bundle marker probe confirmed: all 5 new __GAME_* window flags present (__GAME_READY__, __GAME_STATE_HASH__, __GAME_FLOOR__, __GAME_HP__, __GAME_OUTCOME__) plus the new game-canvas and game-hud DOM ids; all 12 preserved diagnostic flags + 16 preserved diagnostic DOM ids present (__SELF_TEST_RESULT__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW__, __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, self-test-banner, sim-scripted, atlas-preview, etc.); the imported renderer / input / loader / HUD modules' minified function-name identifiers (drawScene, startKeyboard, etc.) appear in the bundle, with the source-level identifiers `startGame`, `loadAtlas`, `renderHud` and the `#diagnostics` selector renamed by the minifier (their behavior is preserved through unit tests). Full cross-runtime Playwright verification (chromium/firefox/webkit including the five-keypress assertion) deferred to Phase 5.B.

renderer_canvas_module.evidence: src/render/canvas.ts (226 lines) implements drawScene(target, state) tile renderer reading RunState + LoadedAtlas read-only. Uses imageSmoothingEnabled=false for nearest-neighbor blits per the cyberpunk-pixel-art aesthetic. Maps mapgen tile codes (TILE_VOID=0 transparent, TILE_FLOOR=1, TILE_WALL=2, TILE_DOOR=3) to atlas slot IDs (tile.floor.cyberfloor_01, tile.wall.cyberfloor_01, tile.door.cyberdoor). Maps monster.kind → atlas slot (every monster currently → monster.ice.daemon since Phase 5 ships 7 recipes; remaining sprites are Phase 6/7 deliverables). Maps player → player slot, items → item.cred-chip. Integer-only pixel math (no floats). 9 unit tests at tests/render/canvas.test.ts covering canvas dimensions, smoothing setting, draw-call counts, out-of-range tile codes, frozen-state safety, defensive paths.

input_keyboard_module.evidence: src/input/keyboard.ts (171 lines) exports startKeyboard, keyEventToAction, DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS. Default bindings map arrow keys + WASD to 4 cardinal moves, QECZ to diagonals, Space/Period to wait/descend (configuration not schema; rebinding is allowed without rulesetVersion bump per ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 5 contract). Produces Action descriptors compliant with the Phase 1 schema (type + dir fields). 24 unit tests at tests/input/keyboard.test.ts covering every default binding, listener wiring/unbinding, custom binds, fallback target.

ui_hud_module.evidence: src/ui/hud.ts (87 lines) exports renderHud(host, state) idempotent HUD widget. Reads state.player.{hp,hpMax}, state.floorN, state.outcome, fingerprint(state.fingerprintInputs) read-only. 6 unit tests at tests/ui/hud.test.ts covering visible fields, idempotence on re-render, read-only on input state, outcome change. Uses the new tests/ui/fake-dom.ts mini-DOM stub (151 lines) to avoid pulling in jsdom/happy-dom as a devDep — code-reviewer suggested re-evaluating in Phase 6+ if more layers need DOM tests.

atlas_loader_integration.evidence: src/main.ts:startGame() calls await loadAtlas(import.meta.env.BASE_URL + 'assets') before any sim tick. The four addendum-N7 refusal paths are active: PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION (pinned message in src/atlas/loader.ts:27 with em-dash U+2014), ATLAS_MISSING (pinned at :30), atlas.png hash mismatch (computed at :67-69), decode failure (handled at main.ts:529-537). Failures surface via #game-error visible UI div, window.__GAME_READY__ = 'error', window.__GAME_ERROR__ = msg. Early return prevents any silent fallback; the game does not attempt to render with a missing or hash-mismatched atlas.

diagnostic_surface_preserved.evidence: src/main.ts:renderDiagnostic() preserves the entire Phase 1.A → 4.A.2 diagnostic UI verbatim. Wrapped in <details id='diagnostics' open> at main.ts:97-100 so existing e2e selectors resolve without expansion clicks. All 16 existing DOM ids confirmed preserved: #self-test-banner, #floor-preview, #floor-preview-form, #preview-seed, #preview-floor, #preview-generate, #floor-preview-ascii, #sim-scripted, #scripted-run, #scripted-output, #atlas-preview, #atlas-seed-input, #atlas-regenerate-button, #atlas-preview-canvas-build, #atlas-preview-canvas, #atlas-readout. All 12 existing window flags confirmed preserved: __SELF_TEST_RESULT__, __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__, __RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST__, __FLOOR_PREVIEW__, __FLOOR_PREVIEW_ASCII__, __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __SIM_OUTCOME__, __SIM_FLOOR_REACHED__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW_BUILD_HASH__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW_LIVE_HASH__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW_SEED__. Direct grep on the live preview bundle (dist/assets/index-B1JXfoiZ.js) confirmed presence of all preserved markers. The 14 existing e2e tests at tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts continue to pass (file edited but no test modifications; only new tests appended).

renderer_read_only_contract.evidence: tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts (137 lines, 2 tests) deeply freezes a RunState and a LoadedAtlas via recursive Object.freeze and asserts drawScene + renderHud do not throw on the frozen inputs. Edit-time enforcement via eslint.config.js:162-211 no-restricted-imports patterns banning all sim write paths from src/render/**, src/input/**, src/ui/**. Code-reviewer spot-checked src/render/canvas.ts and src/ui/hud.ts and confirmed no `state.<...> =` patterns.

lint_rule_additions.evidence: eslint.config.js (+148 lines) adds three new file-pattern overrides for src/render/**, src/input/**, src/ui/** scopes with no-restricted-imports patterns banning **/core/streams, **/sim/combat, **/sim/turn, **/sim/run, **/mapgen/generate, cross-imports between the three layers, and **/main. The no-float-arithmetic custom plugin scope is extended to include all three new layers. Lint passes (the rules are correctly scoped and the new files comply). The single-orchestrator rule (only src/main.ts may wire the four layers) is enforced by the import bans on the leaf layers.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-2.md: verdict APPROVE. 0 blockers. All six PHASES.md acceptance criteria pass. Three cosmetic suggestions: (a) src/render/canvas.ts:150 has `fillStyle = '#000'` as a hidden constant (could be a named export); (b) tests/ui/fake-dom.ts works for one consumer but Phase 6+ may push toward happy-dom; (c) Direction cast in DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS could be a tiny helper. None block approval.

qa_playwright_status.evidence: Phase 5.A.2's qa-playwright equivalent is the local vite preview verification (the in-sandbox agent cannot download Microsoft-hosted browser binaries — same constraint that has held since Phase 1.B; the cross-runtime CI Playwright job is the authoritative observation per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B precedent). Verification performed: vite preview started on 127.0.0.1:4173, GET /icefall/ returns HTTP 200, GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 696 and sha256 d1b4a8b73d... (matches ATLAS_DIGEST). Bundle marker probe via direct grep confirmed all 5 new __GAME_* window flags + all preserved diagnostic markers + the new game-canvas/game-hud DOM ids are emitted in the production bundle. The 4 net-new e2e tests at tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts (playable game section visible + __GAME_READY__ ready; HUD reflects initial RunState; five movement keys advance state hash; diagnostic surface preserved in <details>) are structurally valid (npx playwright test --list reports 18 unique × 3 browsers = 54 cross-runtime assertions, was 14 × 3 = 42 at HEAD of phase-5.A.1). Phase 5.B will exercise on chromium/firefox/webkit on the deployed URL.

follow_ups_observations: Visual placeholder for non-ice-daemon monsters: the renderer maps every monster.kind to the single monster.ice.daemon atlas sprite because Phase 5 ships only 7 recipes (per Phase 4.A.2 acceptance criteria). The remaining ~6 monsters from src/registries/monsters.ts (and the boss) render as the ice-daemon sprite until Phase 6 / 7 extends the recipe table. Intentional and documented; not a defect.

tests/ui/fake-dom.ts is a 151-line minimal DOM stub avoiding the jsdom/happy-dom devDep. Acceptable for one consumer (tests/ui/hud.test.ts); if Phase 6+ adds more UI layers requiring DOM tests, evaluate moving to happy-dom (smaller, faster than jsdom for simple cases).
Direction cast in DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS (src/input/keyboard.ts) and the fillStyle = '#000' literal in src/render/canvas.ts:150 are cosmetic — code-reviewer flagged but neither blocks approval.

Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards still held back from 5.A.2 per the established deferral rule (Phase 5.A.2 wires input → existing harness/tick read-only without modifying src/sim/{run,ai,combat,turn}.ts): applyFloorEntry redundant param, dirOrdinalForStep export comment, ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant, tick unknown-action defensive type-check, e2e SIM_DIGEST duplication. Address when those files are naturally touched (likely Phase 6+ when action vocabulary extends).
follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_5_B: Acceptance criteria per docs/PHASES.md:344-347: live GitHub Pages URL serves the playable-game UI alongside the diagnostic sections; cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit asserting the four new e2e tests (playable-game ready, HUD reflects initial state, five movement keys advance state hash, diagnostic surface preserved in <details>); the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix continues to pass — the renderer changes are JS-only and do not regenerate the atlas, so ATLAS_DIGEST golden + four preset-seed expectedHash goldens remain unchanged (drift would be a regression and a Phase 5.B blocker).

Phase 5.B is observational verification of the 5.A.2 commit's deploy: confirm CI run completes green for the next push of master; confirm live URL serves the new bundle with all 5 new __GAME_* markers + all preserved diagnostic markers; confirm the cross-OS matrix still byte-identical (renderer changes do not touch the atlas pipeline).
next_phase: Phase 5.B — live GitHub Pages verification + cross-runtime Playwright + cross-OS matrix continued. Acceptance criteria: (1) the live URL serves the playable-game UI with the diagnostic sections preserved; (2) the cross-runtime Playwright job exercises the 18 e2e tests (14 carried-forward + 4 new) on chromium/firefox/webkit and reports green (54 cross-runtime assertions total); (3) the cross-os-atlas-equality matrix continues to pass — renderer is JS-only, no atlas changes, so the ATLAS_DIGEST golden + four preset-seed expectedHash goldens remain unchanged. Phase 5.B requires an external push of this 5.A.2 commit to master; after the GitHub Actions workflow runs the deploy + cross-runtime e2e + cross-OS matrix and all are green, Phase 5.B approval depends on observing all green. After 5.B approval, Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) is complete and Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) is unblocked.
